loop_cards.who_has: the OS job of allocating RAM to processes, using techniques like paging and segmentation?
dividing memory into fixed-size blocks called pages and frames?
dividing memory into variable-size, logical blocks?
using secondary storage as if it were RAM to run more or larger programs?
the slowdown caused by excessive swapping of pages between RAM and disk?
a signal that a device or process needs the processor's attention?
the data structure (LIFO) onto which registers are pushed so a job can resume after an interrupt?
the scheduling algorithm giving each process a fixed time slice (quantum) in turn?
the firmware that runs first at start-up, performs the POST, and loads the OS?
the translator that turns high-level source into machine code all at once, producing an executable?
the first stage of compilation, which breaks code into tokens and builds the symbol table?
the development methodology built around risk analysis at every iteration?
the OOP feature where a subclass acquires the attributes and methods of a superclass?
the addressing mode where the operand is the actual value to be used?
the software that manages hardware and provides a platform for applications?

callers.clue: Software that manages hardware and provides a platform for applications.
Dividing memory into fixed-size blocks (pages and frames).
Dividing memory into variable-size, logical blocks.
Using secondary storage as if it were RAM.
Excessive swapping between RAM and disk that slows the system.
A signal that a device or process needs the processor's attention.
The OS code run to deal with one particular interrupt.
Scheduling that gives each process a fixed time slice in turn.
An OS that guarantees a response within a set time limit.
Firmware that runs first, performs the POST and loads the OS.
Software that lets the OS control a specific piece of hardware.
Translates high-level source into machine code all at once.
Translates and executes high-level source one line at a time.
Translates assembly language into machine code (one-to-one).
Combines a compiled program with libraries/modules into one executable.
OS software that copies an executable into RAM ready to run.

quickfire.clue: The data structure (LIFO) used to save registers during an interrupt.
A page is needed but it is currently on disk, so the OS must swap it in.
Pre-written, pre-compiled, tested subroutines you can reuse in your program.
The second stage of compilation, where tokens are checked against the grammar.
A scheduling property: a running process can be stopped and the CPU reallocated.
Platform-independent code (e.g. Java bytecode) run by a virtual machine.
A template/blueprint that defines a class's attributes and methods.
A specific instance of a class.
The addressing mode where the operand is the address holding the value.
The addressing mode where the operand points to a location holding the address of the value.
The XP practice of two programmers working at one machine.
The methodology built around rapid prototyping refined by user feedback.
